---
title: "Blue Carbon Analysis — Step 3"
subtitle: "Random Forest Spatial Prediction"
date: today
engine: knitr
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
if (!file.exists("_targets_rf")) setwd("..")
library(targets); library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(knitr)

cfg                <- tar_read(cfg_rf,             store = "_targets_rf")
stratum_summary    <- tar_read(stratum_summary,    store = "_targets")
rf_models          <- tar_read(rf_models,          store = "_targets_rf")
rf_importance_plot <- tar_read(rf_importance_plot, store = "_targets_rf")
rf_maps            <- tar_read(rf_maps,            store = "_targets_rf")
```

## What this step does

Step 3 uses a Random Forest model to predict carbon stock spatially across the
full project area using remote sensing covariates (the multi-band GEE raster).
Each pixel in the output maps receives a predicted carbon stock value
(kg C/m²) based on the relationship the model learned from the field cores.

**Outputs:** one prediction map per VM0033 depth interval, plus a total stock
map summed to 1 m depth. These maps can be used to identify where carbon is
concentrated and to produce spatially-explicit total stock estimates.

## Model performance

### What good performance looks like

The VM0033 standard targets **≤ 20% relative error at 90% confidence**.
As a rough guide, this corresponds to:

| Metric | Target | Acceptable |
|--------|--------|------------|
| R² | > 0.80 | > 0.60 |
| RMSE / mean stock | < 20% | < 30% |
| OOB error (when n < 10) | Not reliable — collect more cores | — |

**Minimum recommended sample size:** ~30 cores per stratum for a model
that meets VM0033 precision. With fewer cores, the model is fitting the
training data rather than learning a generalizable spatial pattern.
Treat predictions as indicative until more cores are added.

### Current results

```{r}
mean_total <- stratum_summary |>
  group_by(stratum) |>
  summarise(mean_total = sum(mean_stock, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  summarise(overall_mean = mean(mean_total)) |>
  pull(overall_mean)

rf_models$cv_metrics |>
  mutate(
    `RMSE / mean (%)` = round(rmse / mean_total * 100, 1),
    across(where(is.numeric), \(x) round(x, 3))
  ) |>
  kable(
    col.names = c("Outcome", "Method", "n cores", "RMSE (kg/m²)", "R²", "RMSE / mean (%)"),
    caption = paste0(
      "Method = OOB (out-of-bag, all cores used for training) when n < 10; ",
      "holdout 80/20 split otherwise. ",
      "Mean total stock across strata: ", round(mean_total, 2), " kg/m²."
    )
  )
```

```{r results='asis'}
# Interpret performance
if (nrow(rf_models$cv_metrics) == 0) {
  cat("> **No models trained** — fewer than 3 complete cores per outcome.",
      "Add more field cores and re-run `tm()`.\n")
} else {
  best_r2 <- max(rf_models$cv_metrics$r2, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_cores <- rf_models$cv_metrics$n[1]
  if (best_r2 < 0.6 || n_cores < 10) {
    cat(paste0(
      "> **Interpretation:** With ", n_cores, " core(s), the model does not yet meet ",
      "VM0033 precision targets. Current predictions show the spatial pattern of the ",
      "remote sensing covariates weighted by the limited core data — useful for ",
      "identifying priority sampling locations, but not yet suitable for carbon ",
      "credit reporting. **Aim for ≥ 30 cores per stratum.**\n"
    ))
  } else {
    cat(paste0(
      "> **Interpretation:** Model R² = ", round(best_r2, 2),
      " — predictions are approaching VM0033 precision targets.\n"
    ))
  }
}
```

## Variable importance

Which remote sensing layers drove the predictions most?
Higher % increase in MSE means the model depends more heavily on that variable.
★ marks bands with strong theoretical links to coastal carbon stocks in the literature.

```{r fig.cap="Top predictors for total carbon stock. Variables without a ★ label are raw band codes — update BAND_LABELS in blue_carbon_config.R to add descriptions.", fig.height=7}
rf_importance_plot
```

## Predicted carbon stock maps

### By VM0033 depth interval

```{r fig.cap="Predicted carbon stock (kg C/m²) at each VM0033 depth interval.", fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
rf_maps$depth_panel
```

### Total to 1 m depth

```{r fig.cap="Total predicted carbon stock summed across all VM0033 depth intervals (kg C/m²)."}
rf_maps$total
```

---

*Rendered: `r format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")` |
See `reports/step1_nonspatial.html` for non-spatial analysis and simple extrapolation.*
